title: Streamlined application development and user management
html:
We are excited to announce recent updates designed to enhance application development and user management, providing a more seamless and secure user experience.

Unified application development process

The new app creation process addresses a key limitation we had earlier with respect to flows outside of the apps.

Previously, apps created in the development environment could not access other flows like processes, boards, and master datasets that were present in the production environment. Due to this, app developers were unable to reference the data from other flows inside apps. 

Following this update, apps will be created directly in the production environment, allowing direct access to other flows present in production. To do so, app developers will need access to both the production and development environments. 

Once created, a copy of the app will automatically be generated in the development environment for further building and testing. This change ensures smoother integration with other flows and helps maintain a streamlined workflow across environments.

Enhanced user management

Super administrators can map active users in the production environment to the development or testing environments as required. Please note that adding users directly to the development environment has been discontinued.

Key changes

Application sharing: Application sharing, editing, and management exclusively occur within the production environment. 

Unified access: To access the development environment, users must navigate to Profile settings > Environments within the production environment. It is mandatory to sign into the production environment first.

What does this mean for developers?

Developers designated solely for the development environment will be removed from the user management list. Adding them to the production environment and mapping them to the development environment is necessary to reinstate access. Additionally, draft applications that still need to be deployed will now be visible solely in the production environment. Super administrators may share these drafts with developers as needed.

 

These changes are implemented to make the application development and management processes more efficient and streamlined. Try out the new app creation method and let us know your thoughts! Check out the documentation to learn more.

title: Introducing New Enhancements in Kissflow Processes
html:
We have introduced exciting enhancements in Kissflow Processes! These updates allow effective task prioritization with improved visibility and enhanced management of time-sensitive items.

Here’s what’s new and improved:

SLA visibility: View due date indicators directly within the Assigned items section on the web interface.

 

Filters for overdue items: Easily filter overdue items using basic and advanced filters in the Assigned items section.

 

PWA enhancements: 

  • Workflow step filter:  Prioritize tasks by filtering critical workflow steps. 

  • Deadline filter: Sort items based on their deadlines for improved organization.  

Details view: The form details view now includes a due date indicator for Initiators, Admins, and Assignees.  

 

Alert for overdue items: Receive immediate alerts when accessing overdue assigned items.

Overdue and Items due toggle: Quickly access all overdue and upcoming items directly from the process homepage. 

 

These enhancements are designed to streamline your workflow and ensure that important deadlines are met.

title: Introducing guided autofill for process forms
html:
You can now customize form fields with autofill instructions, making AI parsing and autofill more precise and tailored to your needs.
 

 

How it helps

Guided autofill allows you to set instructions to form fields, guiding our AI to extract data from documents uploaded to smart attachment fields. 
 

 

Key features

  • Enhanced data extraction, extract specific information and let AI automatically format, translate, or apply logic to the data before autofilling fields. For example, the AI can calculate percentages on extracted numerical data or translate extracted text into a preferred language before autofill.

  • Field specific autofill control, take more control on how each field in your form is filled enabling fine-tuned autofilling.

Next steps

Start using guided autofill in your forms today. Check out our help documentation.

title: Upcoming enhancements to board views
html:
We’re excited to announce some powerful updates to board views that will take your Kissflow experience to the next level. These features are designed to make your workflows more dynamic, collaborative, and tailored to your needs, all while adding a touch of sophistication to your boards. Here’s what’s on the way:

Expanded view sharing

View sharing is more flexible than ever. You can share a view with individual board users, as well as account users and groups who are not part of the board. You can make them viewers or members for each view. By default, board admins will be assigned as view admins.

This flexibility allows you to bring the right people into the loop, whether they’re part of the board or not.

Inside apps, you can share views with app roles as well. 

Show/hide fields on item cards and layouts

You will have the ability to control which fields are displayed in the layout for each view.

This functionality will be available for both boards inside and outside apps, giving you more control over how your data is displayed.

Field permissions inside board views

Take control over how users interact with various item fields in a particular view by setting permissions for fields, tables, and sections in the item form. You can set the below permissions:

 

Editable - The field in the selected view can be edited.

Read-only - The field can only be viewed and not edited in the selected view.

Hidden - The field will be hidden and cannot be viewed or edited.

New edit permission 

We’re introducing new edit permission while sharing a board inside apps for more flexibility and control.

Revamped view configuration panel

The View configuration panel is getting a fresh look with a more streamlined design, making it easier to manage and customize your views.

Release timeline

You can expect these updates to roll out by the middle of January 2025.

These enhancements are designed to give you more control, flexibility, and ease of use when working with board views. Stay tuned for the update, and as always, feel free to reach out to us in the comments section if you have any questions.

title: Access your datasets inside Kissflow Apps
html:
With this upgrade, app developers can configure app flows to automatically fetch fields and configuration data from datasets while building an app. Once deployed to production, these form fields will dynamically retrieve live, up-to-date records from the synced datasets.

How it helps

If you’re building an employee performance management app and need to retrieve details from your employee PII dataset, simply grant app access to the dataset. Once shared, app developers can easily configure flows to lookup data from the shared dataset directly within the app.

Key features

  • Sync datasets: Dataset admins can grant datasets with app access by turning on the toggle button.

  • Customize permissions: Dataset Admins can restrict dataset access to specific apps by adding customized permissions in security settings.

  • Simulate testing: App users can auto-populate sample dataset records using AI or import data via CSV for realistic app behavior testing.

Next steps

Start accessing datasets inside apps to streamline your app development process! Visit our help documentation for step-by-step instructions.

title: Upcoming changes to blank values in printed forms
html:
Currently, when printing a form with blank values in its fields or table rows, N/A is printed by default. We understand that this behavior may not meet everyone's needs and can be confusing, as N/A could represent a valid value in some cases.

What’s changing?

To address this inconvenience, we’re introducing a small but significant change to enhance user experience. 

Going forward, blank fields in printed forms will remain blank, rather than displaying N/A.

 

Availability and support

This update is scheduled to roll out by the end of the third week of December 2024 and will be available to all Kissflow users. While it’s a minor change, we believe it will bring meaningful value to our customers.

If you have any questions or feedback, feel free to leave a comment below or contact our support team.

title: Upcoming changes to creating lists
html:
We’re streamlining and making some adjustments to how lists are created in Kissflow.

  • The option to directly create new lists will no longer be available.

  • The ability to create lists directly from within the Explorer will also be removed.

However, you can still access lists from the Explorer page under the Managed by me and Others tabs.

 

These changes are part of our ongoing effort to enhance usability. These updates will go live in the first week of December 2024.

title: Introducing the New Kissflow Homepage
html:
We're thrilled to introduce the all-new Kissflow homepage, designed to make your workflow more efficient and user-friendly. This update provides you with quicker access to key information and simplifies how you manage your tasks and projects.

Here's what's new!

 

Streamlined Access: You can now view pending tasks, track items in progress, and easily access recently used workflows—all from a single, convenient location. You don't have to navigate to the "Created By Me" or "Assigned To Me" sections to see your items; instead, you'll find a list of relevant tasks right on the homepage.

User-friendly Design: We have redesigned the homepage to display all activities related to your account directly on it. This includes viewing items with a breakdown of their due dates and workflow statuses, providing you with greater clarity and easy access to monitor each item's status.

We'd love to hear your thoughts on this. Share your feedback in the comments below.

title: Update to managing connections for app integrations
html:
The ability to reconnect connections directly from the All integrations page within apps has been removed. App admins and users will now need to manage connections exclusively via the Connections page within the app for better clarity and streamlined control. 

 

Key changes:

  • The Connect option is no longer available on the Integrations page inside individual apps.

  • Connection management, including reconnecting, must now be done from the Connections page specific to the app.

  • This change will centralize connection management and provide a more consistent user experience.  

This change is limited to app integrations and will go live by the end of the first week of December 2024.

title: Introducing enhancements to document templates
html:
We’re excited to announce new improvements to the document templates, available in processes, apps, and notification settings across processes, boards, and apps. These updates aim to improve user experience, and provide greater flexibility when working with document templates.

Improving table formatting options

Previously, formatting controls for tables were placed inline, as shown in the image below:

  Now, we’ve introduced an intuitive UI for these table formatting options,

  • Column width 
  • Table border
  • Table header
  • Delete table
     

     

Additional table formatting options

We now have six new table styling properties in the template editor:

  • Cell padding
  • Cell spacing
  • Cell color
  • Border color
  • Border width
  • Border stroke
     

     

Added to this users can now:

1. Access a contextual menu by right-clicking on a table, making it easier to manage tables.

2. View last edited information as to when the document template was last updated and by whom.

3. Rename the document template directly from the editor.

4. Text format multiple rows and columns.

5. Resize and reorder cells as shown in the gif below.
 

 

 

We hope these enhancements to the document template feature improve the user experience and offer more flexibility for users working with templates.

As always, we’re here to support you—feel free to share feedback or suggestions for further improvements. Let us know how these updates are helping streamline your work!

title: Rerun
html: Option to rerun a previously completed or failed integration with the same configuration and data.

title: Kissflow Apps
html: Kissflow Apps empowers you to build fully functional applications using a low-code development approach that requires minimal to no coding. With visual tools and optional custom scripting, it enables the creation and deployment of complex, scalable applications with ease.

title: Collaborator
html: Users with whom a connector has been shared. These users can use the connectors in different integrations.

title: Upcoming transition of Kissflow Projects to Kissflow Boards
html:
Update on 21 November 2024:

The transition of Kissflow Projects to Kissflow Boards will happen in February 2025.

As part of our continuous efforts to enhance your project and case management experiences, we’re excited to announce the transition from Kissflow Projects to the more advanced Kissflow Boards. This upgrade will bring you powerful new features while retaining your existing data.

Deprecation of Kissflow Projects

Transition to Kissflow Boards: All existing projects will be seamlessly converted to Kissflow Boards as Kissflow Projects will no longer exist.

Data retention: Your data, including tasks, and roles from your projects, will be carried over to Kissflow Boards, ensuring a smooth transition.

Conversion details:

  • Tasks: All of your existing tasks will be converted to board items.
     

  • Roles: Existing admin and member roles will stay intact, so you won’t need to reassign permissions.
     

  • Project steps: Steps will be converted into board statuses, maintaining your workflow.
     

  • Item watchers: Item watchers will be preserved, ensuring continuity in task monitoring.
     

  • Subtasks: Subtasks will now be managed as subitems within boards.
     

  • Views: All your current views will be retained and accessible in the new boards.
     

  • Project states: States like Active, On hold, and Done will be converted into a custom dropdown field within your board, giving you the same level of control over your project status.
     

  • Settings: Security settings will be retained.
     

  • Custom reports: Your custom reports will remain intact so that you can continue to gain insights from your boards.
     

  • System reports: Some of the system-generated reports will be updated to match the new board metrics.

    • Step summary report → Status summary report

    • Ageing report → Aging report

    • Step performance report → Status performance report

Features not migrated to Kissflow Boards

While most features will transition smoothly, a few will not be available in Kissflow Boards:

  1. Integrations: Existing integrations will not be migrated. You’ll need to set up your integrations newly in Kissflow Boards.
     

  2. Project states: Active, On hold, and Done will become custom dropdown fields.
     

  3. Deadline breached activity: When a board item breaches its due date, it won’t be recorded inside its activity log. Instead, you will get notified when the board item breaches its due date.
     

  4. System reports:

    • Task reports: Assignee summary and State summary reports.

    • Time reports: Assignee performance and Efficiency reports.

    • Flow reports: Cumulative flow diagram, Cycle time histogram, Cycle time scatter plot, Lead time histogram, Lead time scatter plot, and Throughput chart.
       

  5. Audit logs: Your existing project audit logs will not be migrated to boards.
     

  6. Archived tasks
     

  7. Step watchers

Some of the new features you’ll enjoy

  • More roles: Restricted member, Initiator, and Viewer.
     

  • Flow connections: Connect different workflows to create and manage items across them without the need for integrations.
     

  • Timeline layout: Visualize your projects and tasks in a timeline format.
     

  • Customizable notifications: Tailor notifications to keep you updated on what matters most.
     

  • Field visibility configuration: Limit the visibility of fields based on your business requirements.
     

  • Advanced form fields.
     

  • AI capabilities.
     

  • Sort items: Organize your items with ease.
     

Scheduled date

The upgrade to Kissflow Boards will be completed by the end of November 2024 (Update: February 2025).

We’re excited about these changes and believe they will enhance your project and case management experience to a whole new level. If you have any questions or need assistance during this transition, please don’t hesitate to reach out to our support team.

title: Coming Soon in Kissflow: Multi-Lingual Forms
html:
We are excited to announce that we have started working on the multi-lingual forms feature that will allow you to display field labels in your preferred language.

We currently only support language translations of static values across the platform. With this upcoming feature, you can translate dynamic captions created in your forms. This is useful for accounts that need to display the same form in multiple languages.

Of course, the developer has to feed the required language translations into a particular JSON template that is dynamically created for each process.

Please answer these questions so we can better understand your needs.

  1. Besides Labels, what part of the form should be translated? 
  2. Who in your organization can help with translations? 
  3. Do certain users need to see forms in a different language than the one set in their User Preference settings
    Please share your feedback and suggestions in the comments below to improve this feature while it's in development.

This feature is expected to be available in Q2. Do upvote this post if you're excited about this feature.

title: Upcoming enhancements to improve your accessibility experience
html:
We’re excited to announce some upcoming changes designed to enhance your accessibility and streamline your workflow management.

1. A new dedicated page for key menus

To make navigation easier and more intuitive on the home page of each flow, we’re introducing a newly dedicated Manage page to provide you with everything to develop and manage your flow, all in a single place. As a result, the following menus will move out of the More options menu (top right corner) to the new Manage page:

  • Settings

  • Audit log

  • Document templates (for processes alone)

  • External data objects (for apps alone)

Previously part of the settings page, Security will now be a separate widget under the Manage page, allowing for quicker access and easier management.

 

2. Associated flows and integrations

We’re adding two new widgets to the Manage page, allowing you to quickly view and access the looked-up and connected flows related to your flow, as well as the integrations that utilize it.

Update (28 Oct 2024):

The implementation of the Integrations widget is scheduled for a later date, which will be communicated with at least two weeks' notice prior to the rollout.

3. Improvements in the 'Managed by me' tab

Left navigation addition: We’ve introduced a new left navigation pane to help you quickly filter and find your flows.

Integrations: We’ve added a dedicated section for integrations where you can manage your integrations.

 

 

4. Relocated integrations

Integrations will no longer be accessible via your profile in the top right corner.

 

 

Post changes, all the integrations will be accessible from the Managed by me tab inside the explorer. To create an integration, go to Create > Integration, or go to Explorer > Create.

 

5. Relocated ‘Edit’ buttons in processes, boards, and apps

The Edit button on the home page of processes, boards, and apps will be relocated to the new Manage page.

 

6. Availability of lists inside ‘Explorer’

Kissflow lists will be available under the Managed by me tab inside Explorer so that you can view all the lists you manage in one place.

These changes are scheduled to roll out in the middle of November 2024.

We hope these updates will enhance your overall experience and help you manage your flows seamlessly. 

Stay tuned for the update, and as always, feel free to reach out to us in the comments section if you have any questions.

title: Changes to how you access integrations
html:
We have made some changes to how you access integrations in Kissflow, as detailed in the changelog. Here’s what’s changed:

New location for integrations

Integrations are no longer accessible from your profile in the top-right corner of Kissflow. They can now be found inside the explorer.

 

  • Integrations that you are a part of, or created by you are available in the Managed by me tab inside the Explorer.

  • Super Admins can see all their account’s integrations under the Others tab in the Explorer.


     

Creating new integrations
 

To create new integrations, go to Create > Integration from the main menu or, navigate to Explorer > Create and select Integration.

This update is designed to streamline your experience and make managing and creating integrations even more intuitive.

title: Upcoming: Enhanced security with file upload restrictions
html:
To enhance the security to file uploads, we are implementing additional protective measures to safeguard users. This update will ensure safer file management, protect against potential vulnerabilities, and bolster overall data security by restricting the upload of certain file types known to carry risks.

Key benefits of this update

  • Increased security: Helps prevent malicious executable and script files from being uploaded, reducing potential threats to your data and systems.

  • Enhanced compliance: Aligns with best practices for secure file handling and data protection.

  • Proactive protection: Strengthens defenses against file-based vulnerabilities and cyberattacks.

Details of the change

We will be restricting the upload of 72 specific file formats that include executable, script, disk image, installer, and other potentially risky file types.

Note:
This change will be live in 2 weeks from the time of this post.

Restricted file formats

Executable files (23):

EXE, MSI, BAT, CMD, SH, COM, SCR, PIF, APK, JAR, GADGET, HTA, DLL, OCX, SYS, MSC, CPL, OSX, OUT, APP, RUN, EX_, and PRG.

Script files (16):

JS, VBS, PS1, WSF, PY, RB, PHP, PL, JSE, VBE, MSH, MSH1, MSH2, MSHXML, MSH1XML, and MSH2XML.

Disk image files (6):

ISO, DMG, BIN, IMG, VHD, and VMDK.

Other restricted file formats (27):

KSH, VBSCRIPT, PAF, INS, U3P, SHS, IPA, SCT, RGS, COMMAND, SHB, INX, WS, INF1, ISU, MSP, WORKFLOW, CSH, MST, LNK, VB, CAB, JOB, REG, ACTION, WSH, and APPIMAGE.

 

For more information and support, please contact support.

title: Upcoming changes to the app creation flow
html:
We are excited to introduce a streamlined app creation approach with major improvements to simplify your experience. Here’s what’s changing:

Changes in the app creation flow

After this release, all apps will be created exclusively in the production environment. When an app is first created, draft versions of it will automatically be generated in both the production and development environments. The development environment will now be dedicated solely to app building, providing a focused, clutter-free workspace.

Changes in user management

As a super admin, you can now map active users in the production environment to development or test environments as needed. However, adding users directly to the development environment will no longer be supported.

What this means for you

App sharing: App editing, sharing, and management will now be handled entirely within the production environment. Sharing directly from the development environment will no longer be supported.

Unified environment access: Though production and development environments remain distinct, they will function as one unit. To access the development environment, navigate to Profile settings > Environments in production. You will need to sign in to the production environment first to access the development environment.

 

Impact areas

Note: The following apply only to the developers who are part of the development environment but not the production environment.

  • This release will remove users who have been added to the development environment without being part of the production environment from the user management list. To reinstate their access, you must add them to the production environment again and map them to the development environment. 

  • Following this release, the draft apps that have not been deployed will be available in the production environment. The super admin can share the draft apps with the developers from the production environment to make them accessible again.

These changes are expected to roll out within the next week. We are confident they will provide a smoother, more secure app creation and management experience for all users.

If you have any questions or concerns, please don’t hesitate to contact our support team. Stay tuned for the official release announcement!

title: Upcoming: Kissflow Apps will soon access your datasets
html:
We understand that not being able to access records from your datasets in Kissflow Apps has been a limitation. We're excited to announce that our upcoming feature will fully address this!

After this upgrade, as an app developer, when you build an app, you can set up processes, boards, and dataform fields to automatically pull records from your datasets. Once the app is deployed to production, these form fields will dynamically retrieve live records from the linked datasets and lists, ensuring they’re always up-to-date.

What’s changing?

  • Granting dataset access for apps: We're introducing a new option within your dataset's security settings that allows dataset admins to sync only fields and configuration data to apps. This means a blank copy of the dataset, along with its fields and configurations, will be automatically available for use within apps. Please note that once activated, this action is irreversible. 

  • Simulating data in dev environment: All datasets with granted app access permissions will be available on the Explorer page of the dev environment. Users with dataset access can auto-populate sample dataset records and simulate the app’s behavior in a realistic dev setting. Records can be auto-generated using our advanced AI, or you can choose to import data from a CSV file.

  • Using a dataset and a list as a datasource for a field: Following this change, any list associated with the dataset will automatically appear as a valid data source for specific field types. Unlike datasets, live list data used in certain dataset fields will also sync to the dev environment, ensuring accurate testing.

Availability

Stay tuned for the rollout of this powerful feature designed to streamline your app development! Expect updates by the first week of December 2024. We’re excited to announce that these features will be available to all Kissflow Apps plan users, ensuring that everyone can benefit from these enhancements.

If you have any questions or need more information, please leave a comment below or reach out to our support team. We’re here to help!

title: [RETRACTED] Auto-save functionality for process forms
html:
UPDATE on 23 Oct, 2024:

⚠️ Important: Following careful review, we're temporarily withdrawing the auto-save feature rollout for process forms to implement additional improvements. The existing save button functionality will continue as before. For complete information, please see the pinned comment below.

What's new?

We are excited to introduce auto-save functionality for process items in Kissflow. This feature, designed with your convenience in mind, ensures changes are saved instantly as you type or make modifications, reducing the risk of data loss.

Key points
 

Auto-save for process: Similar to what's already available in Kissflow Boards, the auto-save feature will now be extended to process forms, enhancing user experience and form continuity.
 

FAQs:

  1. Will the Save button be removed?

    • Yes, the Save button will no longer be necessary for filling out forms, as all changes will be saved automatically in real-time.

  2. Does this apply to form submission or process editing?

    • Auto-save is only applicable when filling out and submitting forms. It does not apply when editing the structure of a form or creating a process.

  3. What happens if I make an accidental change?

    • If you accidentally modify a form while filling it out, the changes will be saved automatically. However, it’s recommended that you double-check the form before proceeding, as there is no built-in undo option at this time.

Feedback and support

Auto-save is a user-friendly feature that will be enabled by default for all users. You don't need to do anything to start benefiting from this feature.

Stay tuned for the official release announcement in two weeks! In the meantime, if you have any questions or concerns about the new auto-save feature, please don't hesitate to comment here or contact our dedicated Kissflow support team. We're here to help you make the most of this new functionality.

title: Upcoming enhancements to global search
html:
Enhanced global search functionality

We're introducing an update to the global search bar on the homepage to enhance your search experience. Previously, search results covered all time periods by default. To provide more relevant and streamlined results, the search bar will now default to displaying results from the past 90 days.

Note: 
This change will be live on November 15, 2024.

Why this change?

This update ensures that you have quick access to the most recent and relevant content, making it easier to find what you need without sifting through older, less pertinent information.

What you need to know?

  • Default search range: The search bar will now prioritize results from the last 90 days to help you stay up to date.
  • Custom search options: If you're looking for older content, simply adjust the filter to extend the search timeframe.
  • Optimized user experience: The change enhances the ease of finding timely information while still offering flexibility for broader searches.
     

       

FAQs

1. Can I still access older results?

  • Absolutely! You can modify the search settings to include results from All time, Last 30 days, Last 6 months, or Last year as needed.

2. What was the purpose of this update?

  • This change was made to provide a more relevant and seamless search experience, focusing on recent information that aligns with user needs.

Feedback and support

We hope this update enhances user experience. If you have any questions or feedback, please reach out to our support team. We're here to assist you!

title: Coming soon: Home page revamp
html:
We're thrilled to announce an upcoming update to the Kissflow homepage, designed to enhance user engagement and navigation. This revamped home page will make it easier for all users to access key information at a glance, streamlining your workflow and highlighting areas that need your attention.

What's New?

  • Quick access to items: Prominently features your pending items, status of items you have created that are in progress, and recently accessed flows.
  • User-friendly design: Expect an intuitive and engaging interface that ensures all important activities within your account are displayed directly on the welcome page.

     

The new homepage is set to be live in two weeks. For questions or feedback, please reach out to our support team.

title: Item
html: An item is a single instance/task in a process and board.

title: Key
html: The first field, found in the first column, is the key. This field is unique and you cannot rename, delete, or change its field type.

title: Timeline
html: Timeline is a layout type in task management that visually represents items along a horizontal time axis, showing when each item begins and ends in a board.

title: Page
html: Pages help you create interactive interfaces within the app builder that allow you to collect, display, and manage data visually. You can create multiple pages, tailor them to specific user roles, and control access through permissions. Pages enable you to aggregate and present data in an organized, aesthetic format, offering a comprehensive overview on a single screen.

title: Process
html: A process is a structured workflow for getting tasks done in a specific order. It involves initiating items within the process and moving them forward to subsequent steps in the workflow involving approve, reject, withdraw, and reassign functionalities.

title: Document template
html: A document template allows you to automate the generation of process-related PDFs. It's particularly useful for creating standardized documents like offer letters or reports.

title: Run
html: The Run option allows you to preview the app before deploying it to the test or production environment. To successfully run the app in preview mode, the app must contain at least one page and a navigation.

title: List
html: List is a layout type in a board's task management that presents items in a simple, linear format, typically organized in rows, where each item is listed one after another.

title: Table
html: A table acts as a separate section, allowing flow admins to collect and manage data in a structured, tabular format.

title: Editor
html: Editor is a configuration interface where users select and configure trigger connectors, trigger events, action connectors, and action events.

title: Dimension
html: A dimension is an attribute used in analytics to categorize and provide context to the data shown on a chart. Dimensions are usually represented on the X-axis of a graphical chart. For example, in a sales chart, dimensions could include product categories, regions, or time periods.

title: Data Source
html: In Analytics, a data source refers to any form of data from which you can create dataviews and reports. This can be a flow table, child table, or a system table.

title: Item bar
html: An item bar is a colored horizontal bar that represents an item inside the timeline layout of a board.

title: Kissflow App Store
html: Kissflow App Store is a centralized repository that hosts a wide array of applications that cater to different business use cases. Unlike the process and board templates in Kissflow’s template gallery, the Kissflow App Store hosts pre-built applications that are designed and developed by Kissflow specialists with little to no coding effort. After installation, you can quickly deploy these applications for your end users. You can also inquire for new apps that address use cases that are not already available.

title: Model viewer
html: Model viewer in Apps is similar to an entity-relationship (ER) model widely used in DBMS (Database Management System). Model viewer can help you create a real-time visualisation of all the process, board, data forms that are present in the app and the relationship between them.

title: Process instance table
html: The process instance table shows the current status of each item across all processes. This table provides insights into the progress and performance of processes within the account in Analytics.

title: Portal Admin
html: A Portal Admin has access to the Portal settings option under Portal administration. Portal admins can control the portal's default settings that impact how data is formatted and displayed. They will also have access to the user management screen, audit log, and deployment history.

title: Connection
html: An Authentication that enables Kissflow to interact with internal or external connectors using Access key, secret token, or OAuth authentication.

title: Search action
html: Functionality that finds and fetches specific data from applications based on defined requirements.

title: Audit log
html: Audit logs provide valuable insights, enhance security, and help track potential exposure of sensitive data, allowing for better governance and management of your Kissflow account. At the account level, super admins, IAM admins, and user admins can access logs for account settings, user activities, and flow-level actions. For individual flows, process admins can view detailed logs of all activities related to their processes, including user actions and item-level changes.

title: Dataview
html: A dataview is a tabular representation of data created from one or more tables or other dataviews in Kissflow Analytics. It is formed by performing joins or unions via Snowflake SQL queries in the query editor. You can create reports based on the dataview once it's generated.

title: Remote lookup
html: The remote lookup is a field in our form builder that allows you to retrieve data from external systems and integrate it into your forms.

title: Field ID
html: A field ID is a unique string automatically assigned to each field in the settings panel of the form builder.

title: Round robin assignment
html: Round robin is an assignment method that you can choose under assignment preference for distributing tasks in a process. It assigns tasks to users in a rotating sequence. For example, if you have three users, the first task goes to User A, the second to User B, the third to User C, then back to User A, and so on. This ensures an even distribution of work.

title: Legend
html: In the chart interface of Kissflow reports, the Legend appears as a key within the chart area. It provides labels that explain the data displayed in the chart. Users can hover over the chart area to view the data corresponding to each label in the Legend.

title: View
html: A view is a filtered subset of your dataset. You can create views to control access to certain parts of a dataset.

title: Action
html: An event a connector performs after it is triggered. Multiple actions can be configured for a single integration flow.

title: Checkbox
html: A checkbox field allows users to select multiple options from a list, dataset, or dataform in a form. It's a versatile field type that can be used in various scenarios.

title: System tables
html: In Analytics, system tables are automatically generated by the system to monitor status and activities across datasources like processes, boards, and datasets. Examples include Activity Instance, Process Instance, Board Workflow, User and Group tables.

title: Navigation
html: The navigation bar and its associated menu options allow you to define your application's structure for different user role types. You can design your own navigation and assign it to different user roles.

title: Run
html: Each piece of data that passes through the workflow system. Multiple runs can occur when an integration processes multiple items.

title: Goto
html: A Goto is a feature in process workflow that allows you to jump between process steps based on specific conditions. It provides flexibility by enabling forward or backward movement within the workflow. For example, in a travel request process, if an employee doesn't need hotel accommodations, plane tickets, or a travel advance, you can use a Goto to skip those steps and move directly to final approval.

title: Kanban
html: Kanban is a layout type in a board's task management that visually organizes items or workflows into columns, representing different stages of progress, like To do, In progress, and Done.

title: Analytics report
html: Analytics reports allow you to create cross-form reports from various data sources, including process tables, child tables, system tables, datasets, and data views. These reports can be visualized in tabular or graphical formats. There are four types: Tabular reports, charts, card, and pivot reports.

title: Smart attachment
html: A smart attachment is an AI-powered field type in our forms that automatically extracts data from uploaded documents like receipts or invoices. It uses intelligent document processing to map the extracted information to corresponding fields in the form, saving users time on manual data entry.

title: Parallel branch
html: Parallel branches in workflows enable concurrent tasks or conditional routing based on form data, ensuring flexibility and efficiency in process design.The workflow won't move forward until all parallel branches are completed. This feature is particularly useful for processes where multiple teams need to act simultaneously without depending on each other's tasks.

title: Components
html: Components are the building blocks used to create applications in the Kissflow app builder. They include elements like containers, forms, tables, and reports which can be visually configured to define the app's functionality and user experience. Components allow users to design tailored solutions that address specific business needs through customization.

title: Field
html: Fields are the building blocks of a form, used to capture user input within a workflow. You can access the available fields from the fields panel on the left side of the from builder.

title: Aggregation
html: Aggregation field pulls together data from various fields to show relationships and perform calculations. You can use it to calculate sums, averages, or other aggregations of data within a single form or across different processes. You can apply filters to restrict the values used in the aggregation, giving you more control over the results.

title: Initiate
html: Initiate is a type of permission that allows users to initiate new items in process and boards and work on the items they create.

title: Run history
html: Record of all data pieces that have passed through your workflow, showing automated tasks and their execution status.

title: Validation
html: Validation is a field setting available on the settings panel of the form builder. It allows flow admins to add specific conditions to a field so that the form can be submitted only if those conditions are met.

title: Least loaded assignment
html: Least loaded is an assignment method that you can choose under assignment preference for distributing tasks in a process. It assigns the task to the user with the fewest current assignments. This method aims to balance the workload among team members in real-time, preventing any one person from becoming overwhelmed.

title: Matrix
html: Matrix is a layout type in a board's task management that organizes items in a grid, based on multiple criteria helping teams prioritize and categorize items.

title: Multi-select dropdown
html: A multi-select dropdown field is a form element that allows users to choose multiple options from a list, dataset, or dataform of values. It's similar to a regular dropdown, but with the added functionality of selecting more than one item. This field type is particularly useful when you need to gather multiple selections from a predefined set of choices.

title: Custom components
html: Custom components can be created in the app builder’s pages in addition to the the system components provided by Kissflow out of the box. Custom components allow developers to create high-quality app components and share them with others, shortening application development time.

title: Integration
html: An integration consists of a series of sequential steps that transfer and transform data between various Kissflow applications or third-party applications.

title: Section
html: A section is a group of related fields. Flow admins can create sections to organize fields that are logically connected when grouped together.

title: Child table
html: In the analytics module, child tables are automatically created for process and board. They are named in the ProcessName-ChildTableName or BoardName-ChildTableName format. For example, the Purchase Request process generates a child table named Purchase Request Catalog Items.

title: External data object
html: An external data object is a flow in Kissflow Apps that allows app developers to securely access the data stored in external platforms, such as MySQL and MSSQL, without storing the data within Kissflow.

title: Connector
html: Connectors can be services or applications that communicate with each other, comprising triggers, actions, and search actions based on their use cases.

title: Account governance
html: Account governance under account administration is a centralized hub, allowing super admins and IAM admins to audit and manage their Kissflow accounts. This feature is pivotal for maintaining data security and protecting sensitive account information.

title: Geolocation
html: A geolocation field in a form allows you to record users' location data with their consent. This enhances data collection capabilities, providing valuable location-based information for various use cases.

title: Measure
html: In Analytics, a measure is an attribute used to compare data on a chart. It is displayed on the Y-axis of a graphical chart. You can choose to display multiple measure values in a chart/pivot report. Measures are aggregated (Sum, Average, Count, Min., Max., Std Dev) to describe the data.

title: Manage
html: Manage is a type of permission that allows users to initiate new items and manage existing ones. They will have access to all reports by default.

title: Dataset
html: A dataset is a collection of tabular data that can be used in various flows.

title: Step
html: A single configuration unit within an integration that consists of either a trigger or an action. Steps are executed sequentially to complete the workflow.

title: Assignee
html: An assignee is the account user to whom a process or board item is assigned for their action.

title: Board
html: A board is a highly adaptable workflow that lets you keep track of information and manage work efficiently. You can create work items in a board and track their progress across different statuses.

title: Activity instance table
html: In Kissflow Analytics, the activity instance table is a system-generated table that displays the complete step history of each item across all processes in the Kissflow account.

title: Impersonation
html: Impersonation allows service accounts to act on behalf of users or items within a Kissflow account. For example, it can make changes to user details, access all processes and integrations, or perform actions within specific flows.

title: Trigger
html: A trigger is the primary event that starts other actions in your workflow. It initiates the automated sequence of steps.

title: Introducing Enhancements to Kissflow Public Forms
html:
We've introduced new enhancements to public forms in Kissflow processes, allowing you to customize the end-user experience. Here's what's new! 

Post Submission Actions:

You have more control over what happens after a user submits a form:

  • Show Confirmation Message: Create a custom message for users after form submission. You can edit the title and description to provide the next steps or a thank-you note. Optionally, include a button to redirect users to a specific URL.

  • Redirect to URL: Automatically redirect users to a chosen URL after they submit the form.

Customizable Message for Inaccessible Form:

If a public form is disabled, customize the description to explain why it's unavailable. You can also add a button to direct users to your preferred page.

Form Customization:

You can now preview and customize your public form's title and choose a background color that aligns with your brand. Additionally, you can display your account's logo for a more professional look.

For example, when creating a public form—like for event registration—you can set a custom confirmation message for users after they submit their details, providing them with event information or a link to download a ticket.

When registration closes, you can display a message letting users know the event is closed or redirect them to a page with similar upcoming events.

We're eager to hear your thoughts on this. Please let us know in the comments below!

title: Introducing Conditional Visibility for Page Widgets in Kissflow Apps
html:
We're excited to introduce Conditional Visibility for page widgets in Kissflow Apps! You no longer need to rely on custom code to control the visibility of page components. With this new feature, you can hide or show widgets based on various conditions, such as app roles, user fields, device type, or page parameters—no coding required.

Previously, you may have had to work with JavaScript snippets and manual adjustments to achieve something as simple as hiding a widget for a specific role. Now, it's all part of a no-code interface, streamlining your app-building experience and making it more accessible to everyone.

This is another step toward simplifying app creation. It allows you to manage visibility conditions seamlessly without needing external support or technical knowledge.

Read more about this here.

We'd love to hear your thoughts on how this simplifies your app experience. Please let us know in the comments below.

title: Introducing Dataset as a Source for Radio, Checkbox, and Multi-Select Fields
html:
We are excited to announce the release of our latest enhancement - Dataset lookup support for Radio, Checkbox, and Multi-Select fields. For Multi-Select, the behavior will be similar to the existing Dropdown behavior, while the Radio and Checkbox fields will display a maximum of 250 values based on the defined sort order. This feature is also available in child tables.

Imagine effortlessly displaying a dynamic list of values in Radio, Checkbox, or Multiselect fields, which wasn't possible with just Lists. Now, with dataset support, you can create all possible combinations, just like Dropdowns. For example, if a dataset lists all roles associated with each department, then upon selecting a department in the form, only the relevant roles are dynamically displayed. If a user selects "Marketing" as the department, only roles like "Marketing Manager" or "Content Strategist" will appear, avoiding irrelevant options like "Software Engineer." This enhances accuracy and improves the overall user experience.

Read more here. 

We're excited to hear your thoughts on this simple yet significant enhancement. Tell us in the comments below.

title: Introducing Kissflow Workflow Exports
html:
Improve the clarity of your workflow reviews by exporting your process workflows. We are thrilled to announce the new capability to export workflows as PDF or PNG files. This feature removes the need to take screenshots of intricate workflows, consistently guaranteeing high-quality, non-pixelated images.

Consider a scenario where you have a detailed workflow for managing leave requests. Rather than sending unclear, pixelated screenshots to HR, you can easily export the workflow as a PDF or PNG. This lets you share the file with people who aren't directly involved, allowing them to review it without needing access to the workflow.

Start exporting your workflows today and experience a more efficient way to manage approvals. Let us know what you think of this new enhancement. We'd love to hear your thoughts on this!

title: Introducing Kissflow Connector Deprecation and Migration in Kissflow
html:
We're excited to introduce the Connector Deprecation and Migration feature, designed to simplify your transition to updated connector versions within Kissflow.

This new feature allows you to seamlessly migrate integrations that rely on deprecated connectors to their upgraded versions, ensuring uninterrupted performance and better security.

Here's what's new:

  • Automatic Migration: Easily upgrade deprecated connectors to the latest versions within your integrations with minimal effort.

  • Stay Current: Keep your integrations running on the most advanced versions with the latest features and security enhancements.

  • Optimized Performance: Leverage improved functionality to make your processes more efficient and future-proof.

Here's what you need to know when a connector is deprecated:

  • When a connector is deprecated, you will receive an email and an in-app notification to update your integration to the latest version.


 

  • Navigate to the integration builder by clicking on the particular integration that you would like to migrate. 

  • Update your integrations to use the latest version, ensuring optimal performance and avoiding any issues with deprecated versions.

 

If you are using a deprecated version, updating to the latest version is essential. Deprecated versions have a specific end-of-life date and will stop working after that date. Any integration using deprecated versions will be turned off on that date.

Read more about this here.

We've made this migration process effortless. You'll receive notifications when a connector is nearing deprecation and be guided through the transition to keep your integrations running smoothly with minimal downtime.

title: Introducing Image Annotation in Kissflow
html:
Do you need help streamlining precise image feedback that simplifies review processes? Do you wish to edit images directly within Kissflow, eliminating the need for downloading and external editing?

We've added an exciting dimension to your collaborative experience with the all-new image annotation feature, enabling you to give precise and context-specific feedback on images.

  • Customization at Your Fingertips: Adjust the color and thickness of the pen tool to suit your preferences for precise annotations.

  • Shapes and Text Annotation: Add shapes, change colors, apply fill colors, and modify thickness. Write comments, format text, and customize text color.

  • Crop and Rotate: Crop and rotate to ensure annotations are precise and visually appealing.

  • Highlight and Focus: Zoom in and zoom out the image and highlight specific areas, making your feedback crystal clear.

  • Simplified Editing: Select all annotations at once and edit them together for a more streamlined experience.

https://vimeo.com/1000270587

Imagine you’re managing a design review process for a new marketing campaign. You’ve received an image of the latest design draft and need to provide detailed feedback. With Kissflow's new image annotation feature, you can streamline this process effortlessly. Using the pen tool, you can draw precise lines and shapes directly on the image to highlight areas that need changes, such as adjusting the placement of text or modifying colors. Additionally, the text annotation feature allows you to add comments directly on the image, specifying the exact modifications required. This approach eliminates the need for external editing tools, making your feedback clear, direct, and easy to understand, all within the Kissflow platform.

Read more about this here. 

 

Note: This is available for attachments in forms only in draft status, in process comments, and in board notes.

title: Introducing the Delay Connector in Kissflow Integrations
html:
Introducing the newest addition to Kissflow Integrations: the Delay connector is designed to enable you to control the timing of your actions by adding pauses between steps in your integration.

This connector offers three types of delays:

  • Delay for: Pauses your integration for a specified duration.

  • Delay until: Pauses your integration until a specified date and time.

  • Delay on occurrence: Delays based on specific events or conditions.

Let's consider an example. When automating a customer onboarding process using Kissflow Integrations, you can set up a welcome email to be sent two days after a new customer signs up. By using the "Delay for" connector, you can pause the integration for 48 hours before triggering the email action. This ensures that the email is sent at the appropriate time.

We encourage you to explore these new actions and provide your feedback in the comments. Your input is crucial, as it allows us to enhance our integration connectors to better meet your requirements.

Read more about this here. 

Please let us know which delay action you are most excited to configure with this new connector and share your use case.

title: Introducing the SFTP Connector in Kissflow!
html:
We've introduced a new connector to Kissflow Integrations, and we can't wait for you to try it!

Introducing the SFTP connector! This addition facilitates the seamless connection and synchronization of an SFTP server with Kissflow apps. 

To share files using SFTP, users must have a valid SFTP server and be a paid Kissflow customer.

The different actions available in this connector are:

  • Create a directory

  • Delete a file

  • Fetch a file 

  • Update or upload a file

Let's say your organization regularly receives large data files via SFTP and needs to process them automatically within Kissflow. Using the new SFTP Connector's "Fetch a file" action, you can seamlessly pull the latest files from your SFTP server into Kissflow. This allows you to automate data processing tasks without manual intervention, ensuring your workflows are always up-to-date with the latest information.

Note: The SFTP connector currently does not support any trigger events.

Read more about this connector here.

title: Automatically Extract the Month in a Text Field From a Date Field in Kissflow Forms
html:
Would you like to retrieve the month from a date field and display it in a text field?
You can compute this with a simple formula. 

How does it work? 
Sarah, as part of her responsibilities, needs to approve leave requests for her team and conduct quarterly leave data analysis. To facilitate this analysis, she extracts the month from a date field and displays it in a text field using a formula. This allows Sarah to effectively analyze the data and make informed decisions based on monthly reports.

Imagine a form with a date field, Request Date, that auto-populates the date using the 'created' function. To extract the month from this date field and display it in a text field, you can use the following formula:

Requested_Date.toText("mmm") = Jan

This formula efficiently extracts the month from the date field and displays it in a human-readable format, enabling Sarah to conduct her analysis effectively.

You can also extract the day by modifying the formula:
Requested_Date.toText("ddd") = Thu

 

You can also use this formula with date fields where the date is manually input. 

Pro Tip: You can set a default date for a field to show when the form initially loads using this handy formula: date(_created_at.year(), _created_at.month(), _created_at.day())

Read more about the date field here. 

 suggested a use case for extracting the month from a date field. We now use this simple formula in forms where extracting the month from a date field is necessary. This has proven particularly useful for creating reports that include date and month data.

title: Introducing Organization Settings in Kissflow Portals
html:
We have introduced the organization settings to Kissflow Portals to give users more control over their access and enhance overall accessibility.

Previously, portals did not offer the option to assign an item to a specific user, which limited users' access and item assignment. To address this issue, we have introduced portal settings with two major advantages.

1. It allows users from your organization to assign items to users within and outside the organization.

2. It provides access to users from external organizations to assign tasks across organizations.

Here's how it works:

  • By default, the user's organization is detected from the email address. Admins can also modify the users' organizations.

  • To grant users from your organization access to assign tasks to users from your organization and other organizations, add the list of organizations under "Add your organizations."

  • To allow users from other organizations to assign tasks to users from different organizations, enable "Cross-organization user access."

Let's consider a scenario.

Sarah manages two portals, one for Asset Procurement and another for Billing Management. Since she sources assets from multiple vendors who collaborate, she has enabled Cross-Organization user access to allow vendors from different organizations to access and assign items as needed.

However, for the billing invoices, she only requires users from her organization to assign items to vendors. In this case, she simply adds the organizations that permit her users to assign users from within and outside the organization and restricts access to external users.

Learn more about organization settings here. 

As a portal user, we hope this addition makes your portal management easier. We'd love to hear your thoughts on this addition and how it improves your portal usage.

If you're excited about this new addition, please sign in and like this post. Tell us what you like about these enhancements and what could be improved.

title: Introducing Flow-Based Access Control in Kissflow
html:
We are pleased to announce the latest addition to Kissflow - Flow-Based Access Control, designed to enhance integration and authentication processes within Kissflow.

What is Flow-Based Access Control (FBAC)?
With Flow-Based Access Control (FBAC), administrators can whitelist specific data flows, thereby eliminating the need for user authentication when configuring actions in integrations. 

Key Enhancements to Security Settings with Flow-Based Access Control

We have incorporated flow-based access control into security settings, resulting in a complete revamp of security settings across all Apps and the platform.

Permissions for Kissflow Artifacts: Admins can now manage permissions for the following artifacts from the security settings page:

  • Lookup

  • Integration

  • Impersonation

Global Permissions: These general access rules apply to all flows within an account. Permissions are enabled by default for all artifacts except Integration and Impersonation, which can be managed through the Global Permissions section on the security settings page.

Customized Permissions: Flow admins can now set granular controls for individual flows, overriding global permissions when configured. This provides precise control over which flows and fields can be accessed.

Lookup Field Selection: Admins can expose only a specific set of fields to a particular flow, enhancing security and data control.

Apps Behavior: App admins can link apps together and specify which fields from the source flow are accessible to the destination app, improving integration and data sharing.

Integration Flexibility: Integrations with third-party triggers, such as DocuSign and Webhooks, can be incorporated as workflows under custom permissions, enhancing flexibility and functionality. 

Dependency Alerts on Removing Access: When a flow admin revokes permission, a dependency dialog with a warning is displayed to the administrator.

Here's an instance. Sarah, the HR admin at Stark Corporation, uses flow-based access control to securely integrate data from an external recruitment platform into their HR system for employee onboarding. She has customized permissions to only allow the HR team to access sensitive fields such as social security numbers and bank details. Lookup fields like job title and department are selectively exposed to other departments for necessary access.

Read more about this here. 

Your feedback is important to us. Please log in to like this post if you found these enhancements useful, and leave your feedback in the comments below.

We recommend you post your Kissflow support queries in the Get Help section. Rest assured, you'll receive immediate attention and assistance from our support team.

title: How to Fetch Values From a Rating Field in a Number field in Kissflow Forms
html:
Do you use the Rating field in your forms for rating purposes?
Do you know there's an easy trick to fetch values from this field in a number field?

Here's how it works:

  • Create a Rating field.

  • Next, create a Number field and compute it with the field name of the rating field. This fetches the value from the rating field in the number field.

Let's say there's a rating field called Performance Rating. To display the value from this field in a number field, simply compute it with the value Performance Rating field to populate the value.

Sarah utilizes the rating field in her employee appraisal form to assess performance. To translate the performance into numerical values, she has created a number field that fetches values from the rating field. Consequently, when a rating is entered in the rating field, it is automatically converted into numerical values in the number field.

title: Auto-Assign Approvers to a Process Workflow Step Using a Formula

if(Employment_Type= "Consultant",Reporting_Manager,if(Employment_Type="Part Time",Reviewing_Manager,_created_by.Manager))

title: Validate Expiry With Condition Logic in Kissflow Forms
html:
Do you want to auto-populate membership expiries in your Kissflow forms?
A simple condition logic using the IF function is all you need.

How does it work?
Let's consider a library membership renewal form. When renewing the membership, the user must input the membership expiration date in the form. Based on this date, the form fetches details on whether the membership is expired, nearing expiration, or still valid.

- If the expiration is less than ten days away, the field fetches the value "Less than ten days."

- If it is past the expiration date, it fetches "Expired."

- If more than ten days are left for expiration, it fetches "Valid."

Here's the formula you can use to achieve this:

If (RenewalDate <= today(), "Expired," if(RenewalDate < today() + 10, "Less than ten days left for expiry", "Valid"))

The formula checks the membership expiry date against the current date and determines if it expires in less than ten days or is still valid.

Pro Tip:
You can also use the IF function for other condition logic in Kissflow, such as assigning users to specific steps or determining if a step should occur based on certain conditions. This flexibility allows you to automate various aspects of your workflows efficiently.

Read more about the If function here.

By automatically updating the membership status based on the expiry date, you can eliminate the need for manual calculations and reduce the risk of errors.

title: Upcoming Changes: Enhancing Flow Security
html:
Access control policies determine who can access specific flows or perform certain actions within Kissflow. These policies govern access to individual flows.

The Issue

Kissflow currently manages access control for integrations via user’s API-based authentication and authorization, which lacks sufficiently granular control over resources like datasets, boards, forms, and processes. This has led to several issues:

  • Data Leakage: Data from flows can be shared without the Flow Admin's knowledge, making it hard to monitor and govern data usage effectively.

  • Unauthorized Access: Unauthorized users can create integrations using flows, risking sensitive data access.

  • Abandoned Integrations: Integrations created by ex-employees may continue to run without oversight.

The Solution

Transitioning to flow-based access control will provide more precise and secure management of who can access and perform actions on individual flows.

What's Changing?

  • New security settings page: A centralized location for Flow Admins to manage access control for flows. Initially, this page will control access to lookup, integration, and impersonation. Permissions for subprocess and decision tables will be managed here in the future.

    • Global permissions: Flow Admins can set default access control permissions that apply to all flows in the account.

    • Customized permissions: Flow Admins can set permissions for specific flows that override global permissions. They can select specific fields for lookup, with global permissions carried over when customizing new flow permissions.

  • Warnings: Removing or modifying permissions will display a warning message to Flow Admins, listing impacted flows and causing configured integrations, lookups, and related elements to fail if permissions are removed.

  • Behavior within apps: App Admins can link an app and select which permissions from the source app can be accessed by the destination app.

  • Integrations: Non-Kissflow triggers can be added as a flow under custom permissions. Self-triggers and actions don’t need exclusive permissions.

  • Flow duplication: Duplicating a flow or app will not duplicate its custom permissions.

  • Download and Print permissions: These permissions are moving to a different page called Access Controls to manage who can download or print flow details.

Impact and Actions Required

  • Automatic migration: Existing integrations, lookups, and other configured flows using API authentication will be automatically migrated to the new flow-based access control model. No action is required to update these permissions.

  • Configuring permissions: Going forward, Flow Admins can set up global permissions that apply to all flows or tailor access with customized permissions for specific flows. Review and configure these new permission policies and communicate changes to flow creators.

These changes will be rolled out before the end of June 2024. For questions, please comment below or contact support for more information.

FAQ

Q: Will my existing integrations and flows continue to work after this change? 

A: Yes, our team will automatically migrate your existing integrations, lookups, and flows to the new flow-based access control model. No action is required.

Q: Can I still set permissions that apply to all flows, or do I need to configure every flow individually? 

A: You can still set global default permissions that will apply to any flow that doesn’t have a custom permission policy overriding it. See the "Global Permissions" section above.

title: Solving Locale-Specific Currency and Number Format Issues in Kissflow
html:
Do you have users from different regions using Kissflow, often leading to locale-specific currency and number format issues?
You can solve this with a simple formula to correctly interpret the values.

How does it work? 
Stark Corporation, an e-commerce service, utilizes Kissflow to handle order submissions. Customers from different regions enter prices and quantities using their local formats. However, the approvers want to review these in their currency and number formats. By implementing a formatting workaround, the system can accurately interpret and process the values, ensuring precise order handling and reducing errors in financial data.

Imagine a user enters a value formatted in Spanish. Here’s a number input in the form: "565.446.758." This format uses a period as the thousand separator, which differs from the standard US format. Here's an example given below:

The format is not correctly interpreted when using this field in an email integration. This discrepancy occurs due to limitations in handling different formats during integration. Refer to the image below:

 

To overcome this, we developed a workaround using a formula to format the value correctly. Here’s how it works. We create an additional text field and compute it with the following formula to interpret the value in the desired format. 

totext(number(Amount.toText().replace(",", "")), "#,##0.00\;_(#,##0.00)").replace(".", ",")

 

This formula converts the currency and number format, maintaining the correct formatting for the reviewer in both the form and the integration. The Amount field is the field in which the user inputs the value. The Converted Amount computes the value from the Amount field using the above formula. 

This ensures the formatting appears correctly in the integration as well.



Learn more about number and currency expressions here.

 proposed a use case to tackle the persistent problems related to currency and number format within forms and integrations. This customization has been useful in preventing discrepancies and ensuring consistency.

title: Simplifying Kissflow Process Workflow Step Approvals With Common Deadlines
html:
Do you have a Kissflow process with multiple workflow steps and different deadlines, which often leads to delays in approvals? 
You can avoid these delays by setting a single deadline for all approvers in the workflow, which requires them to submit the item by the same deadline.

How does it work? 
Imagine Sarah, the HR manager at Stark Corporation, who handles the employee onboarding process. There are five workflow steps in the process, and to ensure that all approvers have a common deadline, she has a Date & Time field in the form computed with the Submitted at function. She's used the formula _submitted_at + the number of days to ensure the item is approved within the common deadline. 

For instance, she wants the items to be approved within two days from when it is submitted. So, she's used the formula _submitted_at + 2. The number here automatically calculates the value in days. 

Next, she's mapped the computed date & time field to the Deadline field of the workflow steps. As a result, she has achieved a standard deadline for all approvers.

Pro Tip: By default, the assignee will get a notification and email as soon as the deadline is crossed. You can add additional alerts like notifications and escalations based on your needs.  

Read more about configuring deadlines here. 

 suggested this use case to simplify the process of setting deadlines for workflow steps while ensuring a common deadline for multiple workflows.

title: Refreshed workflow builder in Kissflow Processes
html:

Infinite canvas area

New side panel for configuring steps

Indication labels

Unchanged workflow functionality

title: Introducing Card Report for Kissflow Apps
html:
Upgrade your app reporting capabilities with Card Report for Apps, designed to enhance your app reporting experience. With this new addition, you can now create visually engaging cards that represent essential metrics and KPIs.

Our Card Report feature offers four distinct card types: Simple Card, Icon Card, KPI Card, and Comparison Card.

Simple Card:

  • Streamlined and concise representation of aggregated numerical data.
  • Ideal for presenting straightforward metrics with ease.


 

Icon Card:

  • Enhanced visual representation with icons associated with numerical values.
  • Perfect for highlighting key metrics using intuitive icons.

 

KPI Card:

  • Detailed presentation of key performance indicators, showcasing progress from actual values to target values.
  • Users can choose from four different KPI card types to visualize alignment with targets, providing a comprehensive view of performance.


 

Comparison Card:

  • Facilitates side-by-side comparison of two metrics for informed decision-making.
  • Simplifies comparison and aids in quick analysis.


 

With these versatile card types, you can effortlessly communicate essential metrics and KPIs to stakeholders, allowing them to make data-driven decisions.

Here are two examples of how you can use these cards. 

KPI Card: Tony uses the KPI card to monitor Iron Man suit sales. The actual sales volume is 500 suits, while the annual target is 600 suits. The KPI card shows a shortfall of 100 suits to reach the target. The remaining 100 suits to be sold are indicated by a red downward arrow on the card. This helps Tony plan and bridge the gap to meet the sales target. The KPI card provides actionable insights for informed decisions and drives sales success for Stark Industries.

Comparison Card: Suppose Pepper Potts needs to compare the number of leaves taken by Bucky and the total number of leaves for the year. To compare and generate a report, he can include the total number of leaves in the Target value field and the availed leaves in the Actual value field. This will provide a direct comparison of the values side by side.

Read more about this here. 

Note: This is available on the Enterprise plan for App users.
